next_steps: Break down when BeamRemnants kinematics failures occur: histogram offending events vs charged multiplicity, forward energy (|eta|>3), baryon content, and hardest pT; verify these events are not being silently reweighted/reshaped.

Rerun with at least 2 additional nearby seeds (same settings) and compare key observables (Nch, dN/deta, pT spectra, <pT>(Nch), leading-track/jet pT) to assess stability under the high warning/error rate.
Try a neighboring tune/variation to see if issues are tune-specific: e.g., switch Tune:pp or adjust MPI pT0Ref slightly; check whether BeamRemnants and ISR weight>1 warning counts drop materially.

If analysis depends on UE/forward physics, consider tightening/setting a nonzero pTHatMin (or dedicated phase-space cuts) to avoid extreme soft configurations that stress remnants/hadronization; document any bias introduced.
If warnings persist, enable more detailed Pythia diagnostics/logging for the top categories and consider increasing TimesAllowErrors only after confirming physics stability (not just run completion).
risks: Dominant error (42x) BeamRemnants::setKinematics failing after 10 retries indicates problematic remnant kinematics; can bias underlying-event, forward activity, and baryon/strangeness production even if events are ultimately accepted.

Hadron-level failures (2x) and string fragmentation stuck/junction convergence warnings (2+6) suggest occasional non-physical or unstable hadronization configurations; may affect rare topologies and high-multiplicity tails.
Very frequent ISR shower warning (74x weight above unity) can distort radiation patterns and jet/track pT spectra; impacts UE/MB comparisons and any analysis sensitive to ISR modeling.
Energy–momentum mismatch warnings (7x) indicate small kinematic inconsistencies that can accumulate in precision distributions.
MPI 'maximum increased' (3x) hints at phase-space/extrema adjustments; could affect hardest-MPI tails and pThat-related observables.
tldr: 13 TeV pp SoftQCD:inelastic minimum-bias run completed (10k/10k accepted) but shows significant recurring internal errors (beam-remnant kinematics) and high shower warnings, so treat results with caution, especially for tails and remnant/UE-sensitive observables.
